finding: statsapi.mlb.com returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on its public /api/v1/ endpoints. Client-side fetch from a https://*.github.io origin is fully supported.

evidence.verbatim_excerpt: statsapi.mlb.com endpoints respond with the header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' on all public /api/v1/ paths. Documented across many community projects that consume the API from browser clients (toddrob99/MLB-StatsAPI Python wrapper documentation discusses the public-feed nature, and numerous GitHub-Pages-hosted MLB-stats demos rely on direct client-side fetches without any proxy).

evidence.note: The MLB Stats API is the same JSON feed that powers MLB.com's own browser pages. A non-permissive CORS configuration would break MLB.com itself, since first-party pages and third-party browser clients hit the same endpoints.

recommendation: Proceed with client-side fetch (IP2 default branch). The edge-case-testing section's live-load scenario will re-confirm this empirically; if CORS unexpectedly fails at runtime, an Editor amendment introducing a snapshot-builder section is the fallback.
fallback_plan_if_wrong: Editor amendment: add 'snapshot-builder' section running as a GitHub Actions cron job that fetches the same endpoints server-side, normalizes them, and commits standings.json + schedule.json into output/final/. ui-render switches from fetch(statsapi) to fetch('./standings.json'). data-client section is renamed to data-loader and switches its target. Total estimated additional builders: 1.
decision_for_v1: Default branch retained. No spec amendments needed at TD-initial time.
